examples.title: FIFO Page Replacement Algorithm
examples.description: Implementation of First-In-First-Out page replacement algorithm for virtual memory management
examples.explanation: FIFO maintains pages in arrival order using a queue. When a page fault occurs and all frames are full, the oldest page (first in queue) is replaced. Simple to implement but doesn't consider page usage patterns, leading to suboptimal performance in some cases.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n × f) where n = reference string length, f = number of frames
examples.spaceComplexity: O(f) for storing frames in queue

examples.title: LRU Page Replacement Algorithm
examples.description: Implementation of Least Recently Used page replacement using counter-based approach
examples.explanation: LRU tracks when each page was last used by maintaining a counter or timestamp. On page fault, the page with oldest timestamp (least recently used) is replaced. Better approximates optimal algorithm than FIFO but requires hardware support for efficient implementation.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n × f) with counter method; O(n) with linked list + hash map
examples.spaceComplexity: O(f) for frame table + O(1) for counter

examples.title: File Open and Read Operations
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating file system operations from open to read with FCB management
examples.code:
// File Open Operation
FUNCTION FileOpen(filename, mode)
 // 1. Search directory for filename
 inodePtr = DirectorySearch(filename)
 IF inodePtr = NULL THEN
 IF mode = CREATE THEN
 inodePtr = CreateInode(filename)
 ELSE
 RETURN ERROR_FILE_NOT_FOUND
 END IF
 END IF
 
 // 2. Create File Control Block (FCB)
 fcb = ALLOCATE_FCB()
 fcb.inode = inodePtr
 fcb.mode = mode
 fcb.filePointer = 0
 fcb.refCount = 1
 
 // 3. Allocate file descriptor for process
 fd = ProcessAllocateFD(currentProcess)
 ProcessFileTable[currentProcess][fd] = fcb
 
 RETURN fd
END FUNCTION

// File Read Operation
FUNCTION FileRead(fd, buffer, bytes)
 fcb = ProcessFileTable[currentProcess][fd]
 
 // Check permissions
 IF NOT fcb.hasReadPermission THEN
 RETURN ERROR_PERMISSION_DENIED
 END IF
 
 // Translate logical address to physical
 blockNum = fcb.filePointer / BLOCK_SIZE
 offset = fcb.filePointer % BLOCK_SIZE
 
 diskBlock = TranslateLogicalToPhysical(fcb.inode, blockNum)
 
 // Read from disk to buffer
 READ_DISK(diskBlock, buffer, offset, bytes)
 
 // Update file pointer
 fcb.filePointer = fcb.filePointer + bytes
 
 RETURN bytesRead
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: File operations involve multiple layers: directory lookup to find inode, FCB creation for open files, and physical block translation for I/O. The FCB maintains per-process state including file position pointer and access mode. Multiple processes can share FCB through reference counting.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for open (directory search varies), O(1) for read after block translation
